title: Flynn's Encystment Medium preparation (400 mL)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"899nj","text":"Recipe for FEM (a novel encystment medium) which is used to induce synchronous encystment of Acanthamoeba castellanii trophozoites. Results in significantly higher numbers of mature cysts compared to other media (https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvqcn5sw).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":93,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":213,"length":49}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"510267832EB011F0B27A0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvqcn5sw"}}}}
steps: To 500 mL bottle, add: 4.0664 g MgCl 2 x 6H 2 O (50 mM) 3.6032 g glucose (50 mM) 2.9824 g KCl (100 mM) 1.344 g NaHCO 3 (40 mM) 0.7888 g MgSO 4 x 7H 2 O (8 mM) 0.5248 g hydroxyproline (10 mM) 2 mL 20 mM MnSO 4 (100 µM) 2 mL 10 mM NiCl 2 (50 µM) 2 mL 80 mM CaCl 2 (0.4 mM) 2 mL 64 mM AMPD (2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol) (0.32 mM) stir bar
Bring volume to 400 mL with dH 2 O and place on stir plate (unheated).
Measure pH. Confirm that the pH is between 8 and 8.5 (the optimal range for encystment).
Filter sterilize into plastic bottle using 0.22 µm filtration system. Storage in plastic, not glass, is essential to maintaining the pH.
Store at 4 °C for up to 2 months.
creator: Carrie A. Flynn
